content.explanation:
🌐 How the Web Actually Works

When you open a website like cymortech.com, something very fast happens behind the scenes.

Your device (phone or computer) sends a request to a computer somewhere in the world called a server.

The server sends back website files, and your browser displays them instantly.

⚡ Simple Idea

Think of it like ordering food:

  • 📱 You = Customer (Client)
  • 🍔 Restaurant = Server
  • 🚚 Delivery = Internet

You order → server responds → you receive the website.
content.frontend_vs_backend:
🧠 Two Parts of Every Website

Every website has two sides working together:

💻 Frontend (What you see)

  • Runs in your browser
  • Built with HTML, CSS, JavaScript
  • Buttons, text, images, layout

This is everything you interact with.

🗄️ Backend (Hidden system)

  • Runs on remote servers
  • Stores data like accounts and messages
  • Handles login, security, database

You never see it, but it powers everything.

Example: Firebase (which you are using) is part of backend services.

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Website Connection Active

The browser has received data from a server and is rendering it here.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Simulate a live update

1. Add a new <h1> tag below the paragraph
2. Write exactly: Frontend Engine Active
3. Run the code and observe how the page updates instantly
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example:

Frontend Engine Active

Make sure you close your tag properly like

quiz_engine.question: What does the browser do when it receives HTML from a server?
options: Stores it permanently in a database
Parses it and displays a visual webpage
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The browser reads (parses) HTML and converts it into a visual webpage you can interact with.

takeaways: Websites work through a request-response system
Servers send HTML, CSS, and JS back to the browser
Frontend is what users see, backend is the hidden system

cheat_sheet.client: Your browser that requests and displays websites
cheat_sheet.server: A remote computer that stores and sends website files
cheat_sheet.backend: Hidden system that manages data and logic
